title: ipv6_addr.h in6_addr detection may false-negative on unusual platforms
description:
Location: ipv6_addr.h:43-62

**What goes wrong:** The detection logic checks several guard macros (_NETINET_IN_H, _NETINET_IN_H_, etc.) but these are not standardized. A platform could define struct in6_addr via a different header that uses an unlisted guard macro. The code would then define a duplicate struct in6_addr, causing compile error.

**Impact:** Build breaks on unlisted platforms. User must manually define LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR=1.

**Mitigation already present:** LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR override exists and is documented. The comment at line 35-41 explains the detection strategy. This is acceptable but worth noting as a footgun.

Actually, this is more of a known limitation than a bug. The code handles it reasonably with the override. Retracting as not meeting the threshold.
close_reason: Known limitation with documented workaround (LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR), not a bug
